close_reason: Replaced (Client,Uuid,Uuid,Uuid) tuple with named BitwardenSession struct
description: src/lib.rs:75 stores session state as OnceCell<(Client, uuid::Uuid, uuid::Uuid, uuid::Uuid)>. Three same-typed UUIDs are positional — at every destructure site (lib.rs:286 'let (client, _org_id, _project_id, secret_id) = ...') the reader has to remember which slot is which. The two _-prefixed bindings exist precisely because the caller doesn't need them at this layer, but if a future change wants to use _project_id (e.g. for finding 'sibling' secrets in the same project), a position swap silently miscompiles to wrong behavior. Replace with a named struct: 'struct BitwardenSession { client: Client, org_id: Uuid, project_id: Uuid, secret_id: Uuid }'. Named fields self-document and prevent positional confusion. This is the type-design idiom; one of the canonical 'newtype/struct over tuple' cases.
title: Tuple of (Client, Uuid, Uuid, Uuid) cache type is positional and error-prone
